name: Swing 360
description: A 360-degree rotation from the swing, the foundational freestyle bar trick and base for many harder elements.

name: Sklopka (Parallel Bars)
description: A basic kip (sklopka) on parallel bars, requiring more wrist mobility than the bar version.

name: Basic Dismount
description: A trivial introductory dismount (jump off) from the bar, testing composure more than skill.

name: Swing 270 Transfer
description: A swing 270 transfer that starts on one bar and finishes on another, harder than a same-bar turn due to the transfer.

name: Palm Spin
description: A palm spin on the bar performed from a support position.

name: Parallel Bar Elbow Move
description: An unnamed creative parallel-bar move that loads the elbows, best done on wide bars.

name: Giant Swing
description: A full giant swing around the bar, shown from handstand to back using an undergrip.

name: Front Flip Dismount
description: A front flip dismount from the front swing (also called reverse flyaway), kicking the legs and throwing the bar away with the hands.

name: Foot-Catch Transfer Dismount
description: A combined transfer and dismount: swing, place the feet on the opposite bar, then release the hands to land.

name: Reverse Giant Swing (#10)
description: A giant swing (veletoc) performed in the opposite direction, turning the palms into a handstand-like position over the bar.

name: Basic Parallel Bar Dynamic
description: A basic first move for learning dynamics on parallel bars, with the main challenge being catching without touching the ground.

name: Full Palm Spin
description: A harder palm spin that continues a further 180 degrees instead of catching the legs, finishing on the same side of the bar.

name: Rare Geinger-Like Trick
description: A rare freestyle element whose initial motion is somewhat similar to a geinger.

name: Korean Dip Turn
description: A turn performed in the Korean dip position on the bar.

name: Swing Gainer
description: The most common freestyle dismount: from the front swing, tuck the knees, look back over the shoulder, and release into a backward flip to land on the feet.

name: Two-Bar Hand Transition
description: An easy transition in which each hand catches a different bar; can be simplified by keeping one undergrip hand fixed.

name: Double 360
description: Two consecutive 360 rotations on the bar, with the undergrip hand catching either in front of or behind the other.

name: Muscle-Up 270 Transfer
description: A 270-degree transfer performed from a muscle-up, easiest between bars of equal height.

name: Sklopka (Bar Kip)
description: A fundamental freestyle kip on the bar, performed with straight arms.

name: Swing 540
description: A 540-degree rotation on the bar: a 180 into a hand slap on the bar, then a further 360.

name: Parallel Bar Dynamic Move
description: A lesser-known but aesthetic dynamic element on parallel bars, learnable in one session with good bar dynamics, started facing outward at the bars' end.

name: Parallel Bar Roll
description: A roll on parallel bars that visually resembles a flip, gentle on difficulty but hard on the arms.

name: Swing Gainer Transfer
description: A swing gainer that transfers to a second bar, with technique varying by the gap distance.

name: Blind Dislocate Catch
description: Catching the bar with the back turned to it (blind) into a dislocate grip.

name: Swing to Straight-Arm Support
description: A press from the swing up to a straight-arm support over the bar, requiring swing technique and pulling strength.

name: Parallel Bar Hoop
description: On parallel bars, throw the legs sideways from the back swing while turning 180 and regripping, then a further 90-degree turn to catch, like an alley hoop plus a quarter turn.

name: Butt Flip Dismount
description: A backward-rotating flip dismount from the bar, rotating over the head from height.

name: Front Swing 360 (#28)
description: A 360 rotation from the front swing, ideally released from both hands during the turn.

name: Support 180 Jump-Over
description: A 180-degree jump over the bar performed from a support position.

name: Beginner Bar Basic
description: An extremely basic introductory movement on the bar for complete beginners.

name: Over-Bar Body Catch
description: An advanced trick in which the whole body flies over the bar before regrabbing, requiring precise height control.

name: Shrimp Flip
description: A well-known flip relying on precise timing, kick angle, and a specific hand motion to rotate and regrab.

name: Front Swing 270 Transfer
description: A 270-degree rotation from the front swing that transfers to catch the bar, resembling an over-rotated 180.

name: Dip 180
description: A 180-degree turn performed during a dip on the bar.

name: Muscle-Up Double 360
description: A double 360 begun from a muscle-up, caught in mix grip and spun through a second 360 with little swing.

name: One-Arm Swing Gainer
description: A swing gainer dismount performed from a single-arm swing, demanding extra grip strength and stabilization.

name: Parallel Bar Handspring Dismount
description: A front handspring/cartwheel dismount from a support position on parallel bars.

name: Sklopka to L-Sit
description: A sklopka (kip) that finishes in an L-sit position, requiring good hamstring and hip mobility.

name: Backward Sklopka to Sit
description: A backward kip (sklopka) that finishes seated on the bar, usable as a transition between elements.

name: Basic Bar Transition
description: A simple, calm transition on the bar used to link elements without breaking flow, trainable on a low bar.

name: Bar-to-Bar Jump Transfer
description: A transfer where the feet leave the first bar and the body is briefly airborne before the hands reach the second bar.

name: Parallel Bar Backflip (#42)
description: A backflip dismount off parallel bars, performed from the swing or from a handstand.

name: Support to Knee-Hang Jump
description: A jump from a support position over the bar into a hang caught under the knees, used as a prerequisite for palm spins.

name: Parallel Bar Transfer
description: A transfer on parallel bars using the same motion as trick #29 but jumping farther to catch a different bar.

name: Tornado 360
description: A rotational 360 spin performed just above the bar with a strong twisting motion.

name: Kick-Up Arch to Bar
description: A rarely-seen move built from a sklopka-to-L-sit base using a timed kick and a back arch to generate flight before regrabbing.

name: Outside-Grip Sklopka (Parallel Bars)
description: A parallel-bar sklopka (back kip) performed while gripping the bars from the outside rather than the inside.

name: Dislocate 360
description: A swing 360 rotation performed through a dislocate (shoulder-rotated) grip.

name: Cast Flip (Palm Drop Backflip)
description: A backflip dismount initiated from a support/hand position on top of the bar.

name: Backward Roll Over Bar
description: A backward roll around the bar, performed either tucked for slow control or extended with more swing.

name: German Hang 360
description: From a German hang, swing and rotate 360 degrees around the vertical axis while hanging.

name: Lache 360
description: A lache transfer to another bar with a 360-degree turn.

name: Bars Kong
description: A kong-style transfer on the parallel bars performed without using the legs.

name: Dragon 360
description: A 360-degree spin performed with the body held near-horizontal in a dragon position.

name: Bar Fly-Over
description: Pulling and flying up over the bar to catch on the other side.

name: Swing To Handstand (Bars) (#56)
description: Swinging in support on the parallel bars up into a handstand.

name: Back Transfer (Bars)
description: A transfer pushing backward to slot in between the parallel bars.

name: Gienger
description: A back salto with a 180-degree turn, regrabbing the bar.

name: Reverse Double 360
description: A double 360 spun to the opposite side, the first caught in mix grip and the second as a reverse tornado.

name: Kip To Seat Transfer
description: A kip (drop-kip) into a seat with a transfer to the bar in front.

name: Bars Front Flip 180
description: A front flip dismount from the parallel bars with an added 180-degree turn.

name: Side Bar Transfer
description: A basic transfer pushing sideways from one bar to an adjacent bar.

name: Support Bar Hop
description: A hop over the bar keeping the center of mass above it, landing back in support.

name: Muscle-Up Behind-Back Clap
description: A muscle-up with a clap behind the back performed during the movement.

name: Behind-Knee Hang Dismount
description: Freestyle bar trick #65 (see video).

name: Swing 180
description: A swing with a 180-degree turn caught on the bar.

name: California Roll
description: A palm-spin variation performed on the parallel bars (the California roll).

name: No-Touch Pullover
description: A pullover onto the bar without resting the belly on the bar, keeping the hands in one position.

name: Elbow Swing Gainer
description: A swing gainer performed from the elbow pits instead of the hands.

name: Parallel Bar Front Flip
description: A front flip dismount from the parallel bars, angled to clear the bar.

name: Lache 180
description: A lache to another bar with a 180-degree turn.

name: 360 Bar Transfer
description: A transfer from one parallel bar to another with a 360-degree turn, via a seat on the bar.

name: Triple 360
description: Three consecutive 360-degree swing spins caught on the bar.

name: Parallel Bar 180 Transfer To Support
description: A back-swing transfer between two sets of parallel bars with a 180-degree turn, caught in support.

name: Wet Laundry Dismount
description: Freestyle bar trick #75 (see video).

name: Swing Gainer Layout
description: A swing gainer dismount performed in a straight-body layout position.

name: Front Swing Leg Catch (Bars)
description: On parallel bars, from a forward swing catch through and over the legs.

name: Swing 90
description: A swing transfer with a quarter (90-degree) turn onto an adjacent bar.

name: Frisbee
description: A backward swing gainer with a 180-degree turn, regrabbing the same bar (the frisbee).

name: Swing 90 To Support
description: A swing transfer with a 90-degree turn onto a perpendicular bar, caught into a support.

name: Cross-Grip Swing Turn
description: In the back swing, cross the hands into an X to initiate a turn on the bar.

name: Low Gienger Transfer To Back Lever
description: A low gienger on high parallel bars used as a transfer, catching one bar and moving into a back lever.

name: 270 Dip
description: From a dip on the bar, rotate 270 degrees and catch in a hang.

name: Swing Gainer 90 Transfer
description: A swing gainer with a 90-degree transfer onto a perpendicular bar.

name: Shrimp Flip 360
description: A shrimp flip taken directly into a 360-degree spin, caught in mixed grip.

name: Muscle-Up 360
description: A muscle-up combined with a 360-degree spin, caught back on the bar.

name: Parallel Bar Backflip (#87)
description: A backflip dismount from swinging on the parallel bars.

name: Front Swing 180 (Bars)
description: A forward-swing 180-degree turn performed inside the parallel bars.

name: Seated Backward Fall Dismount
description: From sitting on the bar, lean back and fall over the head without the hands to dismount.

name: Front Swing 90 Transfer
description: A forward-swing transfer with a 90-degree turn onto a perpendicular bar.

name: Parallel Bar Slide
description: A sliding move along the length of the parallel bars.

name: Lache
description: Swing and release from one bar to catch another bar.

name: Knee Giant
description: A full giant swing around the bar hooked by the backs of the knees, without using the hands.

name: Instep-Hang Half Front Flip
description: From hanging by the tops of the feet, perform a half front somersault to dismount.

name: Double Palm Spin
description: An advanced palm spin combining the parallel-bars palm spin with the high-bar palm spin in one continuous movement.

name: Forearm Roll 360
description: A forearm roll on the bar followed immediately by a 360-degree spin.

name: Forearm Roll To Feet
description: From a forearm roll, transition into a jump landing on the feet on the bar.

name: Forward Roll To Support
description: A forward roll on the bar without releasing the grip, finishing in a straight-arm support.

name: Double-Bar Jump To Support
description: A jump clearing two bars in one leap on double parallel bars, landing in a support position.

name: Partner Swing Gainer
description: A swing gainer dismount performed from a second person's feet instead of the bar.

name: Support Butt Flip
description: From a support position, release the hands and flip backward to catch under the knees, a variation of the seated butt flip.

name: Back Hip Circle
description: Swing the legs back, bring the pelvis to the bar and let momentum carry the body around backward.

name: Swing Gainer 180 Dismount
description: A backward swing gainer with a 180-degree rotation performed as a dismount.

name: Kong Transfer To Bar
description: From a support position, tuck the legs between the arms and kong across to another bar.

name: Overhand Knee-Hang Transition
description: A transitional bar move caught into a behind-the-knee (knee-pit) position using an overhand grip, used to set up moves like the palm spin.

name: Sklopka 180 to Elbow Position
description: A drop-swing (sklopka) on parallel bars with a 180-degree turn ending in an elbow-support position with the arms extended forward.

name: Cast Flip with Leg Thread-Through
description: A cast flip dismount where the legs are first threaded through the hands from a seated position before the flip.

name: Arm-Strength Uprise (Seated Start)
description: A harder variant of trick 6 starting seated on the bar with the legs outside, pulling up into support using only arm strength.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 109
description: Freestyle bar trick #109 (see video); a transitional move usable between other elements in a dynamic set.

name: Foot-Assisted Side-to-Inside Transition
description: Transitioning from the side of the parallel bars to the inside using an active foot push, pressing on the underhand-gripped arm.

name: Giant Swing X-Grip
description: A giant swing (veletoc) variation with a hand regrab into a crossed X grip.

name: Bar-to-Bar Transfer (#112)
description: A basic way to move from one set of parallel bars to an adjacent set.

name: Reverse Bar Slide
description: A backward sliding move along the parallel bars, the reverse of the forward slide.

name: Front Swing 360 (#114)
description: From a classic swing, turning 360 degrees in the front swing phase to finish in a dislocate position.

name: Double 360 X-Grip
description: A double 360 rotation on the bar caught in a crossed (X) overhand grip.

name: Half-Circle Leg Swing (Bars)
description: Swinging on parallel bars, leaning over one hand and circling the legs in a half-circle around the released hand.

name: Foot-Push Bar Vault
description: A support vault where the feet plant on the bar tube and push off to rebound back.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 118
description: Freestyle bar trick #118 (see video); a very easy beginner warm-up move.

name: Seated Underhand 180 (Bar)
description: From a seated position on the bar with an underhand grip, flying over the bar in a back-uprise position and performing a 180.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 120
description: Freestyle bar trick #120 (see video); a combination joining tricks #116 and #118.

name: Palm Spin 540 Dismount
description: A palm-spin-based dismount on parallel bars with a 540-degree spin while hopping over one bar.

name: Swing Cast Flip
description: A cast flip dismount performed from a swing rather than from a support, requiring precise timing.

name: Frontflip from Support (Bar)
description: A front flip dismount off the bar performed from a support position.

name: Front Swing 180 Transfer
description: A 180-degree turn in the front swing phase performed as a transfer to the adjacent bars.

name: Dislocate Uprise to Support
description: Continuing an uprise all the way to a support with one hand underhand and the other in a dislocate grip.

name: Kid Tarzan
description: A backward uprise transition on the bar (Kid Tarzan) swinging up from the back swing to pop the feet forward and rise into support.

name: Swing to Knee-Hang Release
description: Generating power from a swing, lifting the legs and releasing both hands to lean back and catch the bar by the backs of the knees.

name: Straddle (Roznozka)
description: A gymnastics-style straddle on parallel bars requiring strong dip strength to throw and catch the body.

name: Knee-Hang 180
description: From a hang by the knees, swinging and performing a 180-degree turn to bring the legs back underneath.

name: Tornado 360 Variant
description: A 360-degree tornado-style rotation on the bar starting from a dislocate-grip swing position.

name: Backflip Regrab (Bars)
description: A backflip between the parallel bars caught as a regrab, landing on the forearms or biceps.

name: Dip 270 from Elbow Support
description: A 270-degree dip rotation performed from an elbow-support position between the bars rather than from a support.

name: Leg Thread-Through (Bar)
description: Threading the legs through the arms while on the bar, similar to a shrimp flip but performed without gravity assistance.

name: Pull-Up 180 to Back Lever
description: A pull-up with a 180-degree turn transitioning into a back lever, one hand finishing underhand.

name: Elbow Spin to Support
description: Spinning on the parallel bars in an elbow-support position and then regrabbing onto the hands.

name: Sklopka to Elbow Support
description: A drop-swing (sklopka) on parallel bars caught into an elbow-support position rather than a full support.

name: Kid Tarzan to Leg-Through Combo
description: A combination linking a Kid Tarzan uprise with a leg-through transition while keeping both hands on the bar.

name: 180 Leg-Over to Sklopka Combo
description: A sequence of a 180-degree turn with a leg throw, a backward drop, a knee-hooked drop-swing, and a leg throw regrabbing into support.

name: Straight-Body 90 Transfer
description: A transfer catching a single bar with a 90-degree turn achieved by placing the hands to the side while the body stays straight.

name: Straight-Body 90 Transfer (High Bar)
description: A single-bar turning transfer performed on a high bar with an added quarter turn, done higher off the ground.

name: Seat Flip 180
description: Performing a seat flip (prdelflip) off the bar with an added 180-degree turn.

name: Handspring Regrab
description: Starting like a front handspring over the bar but regrabbing the hands mid-move to control rotation and avoid falling.

name: Palm Spin to Adjacent Bars
description: A palm spin on parallel bars that travels across into the middle of the adjacent set of bars, ending with a rotated wrist grip.

name: Hop to Elbow Support
description: Hopping up and transitioning the legs through into an elbow-support position between the bars.

name: Legs Muscle-Up (Knee Hang to Sit)
description: From a knee hang, swinging up into a seated position on the bar without using the hands, like a muscle-up performed on the legs.

name: Swing to Handstand Press
description: A controlled swinging press up into a handstand, stopping precisely in the handstand position.

name: Swing Gainer Bar Kick
description: Performing a swing gainer while intentionally kicking one foot against the bar.

name: Underhand Swing-Over (Bars)
description: A basic parallel-bar transition swinging over with one hand kept in an underhand grip throughout.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 149
description: Freestyle bar trick #149 (see video); a twisting move that is easier for those skilled at twisting rotations.

name: Sklopka 180 Transfer
description: Releasing mid-sklopka and turning 180 degrees while traveling backward to catch the bar behind.

name: Swing Gainer Double Bar Kick
description: During a swing gainer, kicking both feet against the bar.

name: Swing Leg-Over (Nalet)
description: Throwing the legs over the bar during a swing to land the feet on the bar.

name: Lache 180 to Sit Transfer
description: Performing a lache between bars with an added 180-degree turn and height to land seated on the opposite bar.

name: Swing to Handstand (Bars) (#154)
description: Swinging over a parallel bar all the way up into a handstand with a quarter turn and hand regrab.

name: Elbow-Hang Backflip Dismount
description: From a hang by the elbows behind the back, swinging and performing a flip dismount off the bar.

name: Butt-Mount Leg Swap
description: Throwing the legs to the side to sit the hips on the bar, then using the momentum to throw the legs back over to the other side.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 157
description: Freestyle bar trick #157 (see video).

name: Bar-to-Bar Handstand Transfer
description: Transferring between parallel bars while swinging into a handstand and regrabbing the hands upside down.

name: Korean Dip 180
description: From a Korean dip position on parallel bars, swinging the body around 180 degrees.

name: Sklopka Transfer to Sit 90
description: Transferring between parallel bars from a drop-swing (sklopka) into a seated position with a 90-degree turn.

name: Underhand Giant Swing Release Regrab
description: An underhand giant swing that releases both hands at the top and re-catches the bar.

name: Bar Hop (One Hand)
description: An easier bar hop over the bar that keeps one hand on throughout for a small controlled swing.

name: Through-the-Gap (Double Bars)
description: A move that squeezes through the narrow gap between double bars, treating one bar as an obstacle to clear.

name: 360 (Knee/Elbow Hook)
description: A 360-degree rotation using the backs of the knees and elbows, starting close to the bar.

name: Half-Twist Dismount
description: The #3 dismount with an added 180-degree twist, a good prep for full twisting technique.

name: Roll Transfer (Bars)
description: A forward-roll transfer from support on one parallel bar, releasing to catch the other bar and pressing back to support.

name: Knee-Hang Spin Up
description: A spin on the bar hanging by the backs of the knees, hooking the legs and leaning back with straight arms to rise on top.

name: Elbow-Hook Catch (Bars)
description: Catching the bar behind by hooking the elbows instead of the hands, following the #166 setup.

name: Korean Dip 270
description: A korean dip transfer rotating 270 degrees to catch the adjacent bar while staying vertical.

name: Back Roll on Parallel Bars
description: A backward roll along the parallel bars, with crossed hands aiding the rotation.

name: Knee-Hook Catch (Wide Bars)
description: A knee-hang catch where, with wider-spaced bars, the hands release and the body briefly flies back before the knees hook.

name: Over-Bar 90 Transfer
description: A transfer over the bar and then 90 degrees to the side onto another bar, mechanically close to a bar hop.

name: Giant Swing Cast Flip
description: A dismount combining a giant swing with a cast flip.

name: Muscle-Up 90 Transfer
description: A 90-degree muscle-up transfer between bars.

name: Transfer to 90 Degrees
description: A transfer into a 90-degree position from a seated or knee-hang start, swinging high enough to sight the side bar.

name: Shrimp Flip (Seated Entry)
description: A shrimp flip variation started from a seated, L-sit or V-sit on top of the bar, falling back to reach the flip position.

name: Knee-Swing Transfer to Korean Dip
description: A bar-to-bar transfer swinging on the knees and catching blind behind the back into a korean dip.

name: Jump to Dislocate
description: A basic jump transition into a dislocate grip position, releasing while turned back-to-ground.

name: Palm Spin on Forearm
description: A palm spin variation that pivots on the forearm instead of the palm, making the balance point harder to hold.

name: Front Flip 180
description: A front-flip dismount with an added 180-degree twist.

name: Korean Dip 0
description: A korean dip that goes straight forward to the bar in front with no side rotation, suited to cramped setups.

name: Geinger With Leg
description: A geinger variation that braces a leg against the bar to make the rotation easier.

name: Seated 360 Flow
description: A harder variation of #156 adding a 360-degree rotation performed on the seat rather than in the air.

name: Butt Flip 360
description: A butt-flip dismount rotating 360 degrees on both the vertical and horizontal axes.

name: Explosive Behind-Back Muscle-Up
description: A powerful explosive muscle-up variation building on the behind-the-back clap muscle-up, useful for pull-up explosivity.

name: Forearm-Start Press-Up
description: A basic parallel-bar press-up (variation of #11) started from the forearms, requiring a bigger push to rise.

name: Quad 360
description: Four consecutive 360-degree rotations on the bar, requiring fast rotation and air coordination.

name: Rare Back-Over Bar Catch
description: Falling backward over the head into a specific bar catch that requires good shoulder mobility.

name: Forearm Bar Jump
description: A parallel-bar jump transition (variation of #77) supported on the forearms rather than the hands.

name: Back Uprise Grip Switch
description: A back uprise with a brief flight phase that switches the grip from overhand to underhand mid-air.

name: Uprise Over Bar to Dislocate
description: A back uprise carried over the bar into a dislocate position, requiring extra power and well-stretched shoulders.

name: Swing Gainer 360
description: A swing gainer dismount with a full 360-degree twist.

name: Reversed Knee-Hang Swing
description: The #177 transfer performed in reverse, relying on hooking the knees and swinging in the opposite direction.

name: Legs-Out Dismount Regrab
description: A variation of the #65 dismount turned into a regrab, staying close enough to re-catch the bar while still pushing the legs out.

name: Seated Leg-Through Push
description: Threading the legs backward past the bar from a seated position, combining tricks 57 and 133 and demanding shoulder mobility and compression.

name: Seated Spin to Hang
description: A spin on the seat starting from throwing the legs over the bar and bracing on the other bar, ending in a hang on the starting bar.

name: Seated 270 to Support
description: A 270-degree spin on the seat that ends in a support position on the parallel bars.

name: 180 Support to Back Lever
description: A 180 from support, catching the bar behind the knees to drop into a back lever.

name: Support to Knee-Hang 90 Transfer
description: From support, turning 90 degrees and catching the adjacent bar under the knees with a brief moment of flight.

name: Seated Weight Transfer to Bar
description: A seated variation that stays on the seat longer to transfer body weight onto the arm on the opposite bar.

name: Fake One-Arm 540
description: A 540-degree spin performed in a one-arm style, requiring strong grip.

name: Shrimp Flip 180 Entry
description: A shrimp flip variation entered from a 180-degree turn with a re-grip of both hands before dropping under the bar.

name: Layout Backflip Between Bars
description: A layout backflip that lands between the two parallel bars.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 204
description: Freestyle bar trick #204 (see video); a power-based warm-up move requiring a back kip.

name: Supra Swing to Sit
description: Generating a large swing in the supra position from a seated push-off, then pulling through to return to sitting on the bar.

name: Seated Spin (Static Transition)
description: A spin on the seat used to transition between static holds such as front lever and planche.

name: Knee-Hang Swing Dismount
description: Generating a swing from a hang by the backs of the knees and dismounting.

name: 180 on Parallel Bars (Prep)
description: A 180-degree rotation on the parallel bars, an easier prep version of the full variant that lands in a support catch.

name: Swing 270 Dislocate Transfer
description: A front swing 270 variation with a shoulder dislocate that catches the adjacent bar blind, hands up while looking toward the ground.

name: Support Weight-Shift Transition
description: A foundational transition shifting body weight over the non-releasing supporting arm on the bars.

name: Knee-Jump Spin Regrab
description: From a jump onto the knees, spinning above the bar while switching grip and re-catching the bar behind the legs.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 212
description: Freestyle bar trick #212 (see video).

name: Layout Butt Flip
description: A butt-flip dismount performed in a straight, arched layout position with a push up to height before rotating.

name: Seated Cheat 450 Spin
description: A cheat rotation on the parallel bars that briefly sits on the bar, entered via a prior spin for roughly 450 degrees of total rotation.

name: Knee-Hang Mini-Swing Dismount
description: A dismount from a hang by the knees using a tiny swing so the feet land behind the bar while the torso stays roughly in place.

name: One-Leg Kip
description: A single-leg variation of the basic kip (sklopka), physically easier but more coordination-dependent.

name: Easy Bar Frontflip
description: A technically easier version of the classic frontflip dismount from the bar, requiring mobile shoulders.

name: Stand-Up on Bar
description: A basic element of standing up to balance on the bar.

name: Giant Palm-Release Regrab
description: A giant swing in which the palms rotate over to handstand, push off and release, then regrab the bar.

name: Forearm Bar-Jump Entry
description: A variation of jumping into the parallel bars, leaning forward and landing directly on the arms instead of rising through the shoulders.

name: Seated Frontflip Regrab
description: Performing the trick-217 front rotation from a seated start for more swing and catching the bar instead of dismounting.

name: Hefesto-Entry Transfer
description: A mirror-image variation of trick #133, moving the legs through to reach a hefesto starting position and avoid stepping over the bar.

name: X-Grip Swing Gainer
description: A harder variation of the classic swing gainer performed with an X (crossed) grip.

name: Back Roll to Straddle Sit
description: A backward roll on the bars, spreading the legs while inverted to end sitting astride the bars.

name: Reverse Korean Spin
description: A dynamic reverse of the classic Korean turns, essentially the movement played backwards.

name: Cheat 360
description: A cheat 360 performed from support to support, a prep for the full version.

name: No-Swing Gainer
description: A gainer flip performed from a hang without swinging, requiring extra speed and explosiveness.

name: Front Roll to Sit
description: A front roll on the parallel bars, staying longer on the elbow pockets and swinging out to end seated on one bar.

name: No-Hands Twist
description: A twisting move similar to trick #149 but performed without the hands on the bar.

name: Palm Spin to Under-Knees
description: A rare palm spin variation on the parallel bars sent toward the far bar to land under the knees.

name: Backward Tip to Bar Hang
description: A two-phase move on the bars: tipping backward off a leg push, then transferring from the elbows into a hang on the second bar.

name: Unconventional P-Bar Trick
description: Freestyle bar trick #232 (see video).

name: Hands-Free Under-Knees Drop
description: Falling head-down without the hands to land the legs precisely under the knees on the bar.

name: 180 to Dislo
description: Turning 180 degrees and re-catching into a dislocate grip once the back faces the bar.

name: No-Twist Swing Gainer
description: A swing gainer variation performed without a twist, usable as a prep for the frisbee.

name: Lumbar Rotation Prep
description: A basic preparatory movement requiring coordination and lumbar-spine rotational flexibility, used as a prerequisite for harder variations.

name: Back Roll Leg-Cross
description: A variation of the backward roll on the bars performed with the legs crossed.

name: Back Circle to Giant
description: A combination of a backward circle and a classic giant swing, requiring a tight hollow start and switching the hands over the bar.

name: Split 540
description: A 540 performed as a 180 in one direction followed by a 360 in the other, from a bar swing.

name: Under-Knees Jump with Extra Turn
description: A jump to an under-the-knees hang with an added rotation, pushing off a foot placed on the bar and requiring balance.

name: Korean Roll-Over Transfer
description: A transfer derived from the Korean dip roll, rolling from one side to the other like turning over in bed.

name: Dip 225
description: A dip transfer rotating 225 degrees, technically between a Korean dip 180 and a dip 270.

name: Cork Zero Transfer
description: An off-axis cork rotation performed as a transfer between bars.

name: Taco Bar Transfer
description: A transfer to the opposite bar driven by a leg kick and pull, ending folded over the bar; a gentler relative of the shrimp flip.

name: X-Grip Geinger
description: A geinger performed with an X (crossed) grip, staying straight through the swing to keep the crossed grip from twisting.

name: Parkour Mount
description: A parkour-inspired mount onto the bar, jumping over the shoulder from an unusual hand placement that stays in contact with the bar.

name: Giant to Leg-Assisted Geinger
description: A giant swing transitioning directly into a leg-assisted geinger, with precise placement of the leg on the bar.

name: Knee-Swing Transition
description: A transitional movement swinging on the knees, with frequent hand regrips.

name: 270 Twist to Regrab
description: A 270-degree twist to regrab performed while swinging on the knees, in a corner setup.

name: Follower-Requested Bar Trick
description: Freestyle bar trick #250 (see video).

name: Swing Gainer 180
description: A dismount consisting of a swing gainer with an added 180-degree turn, distinct from a geinger.

name: Squat-Power P-Bar Trick
description: A rare p-bar movement requiring squat strength, usable mainly as a combo opener.

name: Leg Circle to Catch
description: A full leg circle around the bar while the torso stays in place, ending in a hanging catch.

name: Knee-Kip to Bar Top
description: Using the knees on the front bar to generate momentum, then releasing and folding the legs through the arms to get on top of the bar.

name: Spider Muscle-Up
description: A muscle-up variation using open-leg drive, sometimes referred to as a spider muscle-up.

name: P-Bar Creative Transition
description: A creative transitional movement on the parallel bars.

name: Cancel Geinger
description: An off-axis inverted rotation resembling a reversed geinger, informally called a cancel geinger or upside-down 180.

name: One-Leg Spin Dismount
description: A dismount off the end of the p-bars using one leg for takeoff and the other to drive the spin.

name: Prdelflip Stand-Up Variation
description: A harder variation of the prdelflip (seat flip) used to arrive standing on the bar.

name: P-Bar Transfer
description: A simple, effective transfer between parallel bars.

name: Support Front-Roll Dismount
description: An easy dismount from a basic support, generating power with the arms as in a front roll before releasing the bar.

name: Elbow-Catch Transfer
description: A p-bar transfer using extra swing to reach and catch the second bar with the elbows.

name: Korean Dip Roll Transfer
description: A basic Korean dip roll used to transfer across to a second bar.

name: Creative Bar-Sit Transition
description: A creative transitional movement on the bars that ends seated on the bar.

name: Supra 180 Transfer
description: A supra 180 with an added 90-degree twist, turning the move into a transfer between bars.

name: Easy 360 Leg-Swing Spin
description: The easiest 360 spin, started by swinging a leg over the bar and leaning forward to let momentum carry the rotation.

name: Double 360 Mixed Grip
description: A double 360 with one rotation on each side, the first caught in a reversed mixed grip.

name: 360 Seated Spin Variation
description: A 360-degree spin performed seated on the bars, caught with the legs still pointed out while the chest turns toward the bar.

name: Forearm Front Handspring
description: A front handspring on parallel bars performed on the forearms instead of the hands.

name: Bar-to-Bar Off-Axis Flip
description: An off-axis flip performed from one bar to another, tapping the target bar with the hand mid-rotation before catching.

name: Twisting Drop
description: A move that is essentially a controlled fall from the bar with a bit of twist, good for coordination.

name: Bar to Bar Transfer (#272)
description: A transfer move moving from one bar to another.

name: Korean Dip Roll Variation
description: An innovated variation of the oldschool korean dip roll (trick #14).

name: Thigh-Support P-Bar Move
description: A parallel-bar move staying above the right arm and using the thigh as support on the bar.

name: Supra Frontflip Regrab To Side
description: A supra frontflip regrab variation catching the bar to the side instead of straight ahead.

name: Dragon 360 Swing 180 Mix
description: A mix of a dragon 360 and a swing 180 done from a p-bar front roll, tucking the legs during the spin.

name: Kick Grab Release
description: A simple move kicking the legs up, grabbing them, and releasing moments later.

name: Lache 180 to Mixed Grip
description: A hard 180 move that can be built from a lache with a 180 to mixed grip, going progressively more upside down.

name: P-Bar Trick 274 With 90
description: An easier variation of trick #274 with an added 90-degree spin, leaning on one parallel bar.

name: Leaping Frog Transfer
description: A single-bar move turned into a bar-to-bar transfer resembling a leaping frog motion.

name: Fear-Factor Bar Landing
description: A bar move rated mainly for its fear factor, requiring somewhat strong legs to endure the landing.

name: Butt Spin 360
description: A parallel-bar move sitting on the butt (trick #30) taken to a full 360-degree spin.

name: Trick 261 With 180
description: A harder variation of trick #261 adding an extra 180 degrees of rotation.

name: Lean-In Bar Transition
description: A bar transition where leaning further toward the bar makes it easier and more controlled.

name: Leg Hang Transfer Regrab
description: A move relying on squeezing the legs to hang, whose second phase is trick #145 but regrabbing the starting bar.

name: Shrimp 180 Mixed Grip
description: An easier shrimp 180 variation started in a mixed grip so neither hand leaves the bar.

name: Shrimp Zero
description: A shrimp move doing a 180 in one direction and then 180 back to the original position.

name: Progression One Bar Trick
description: The easiest of a three-trick progression series, leading toward tricks #279 and #274.

name: Straddle Bar Element
description: A straddle-leg variation of a static bar element on a wide bar, usable as an entry into a shrimp flip.

name: No Swing 360
description: A 360-degree turn around the bar performed without swing, also called a pull-up 360.

name: P-Bar Cheat Kip
description: A parallel-bar variation of the basic bar kip, using the bar width to kip up from a hang.

name: Reverse Giant Swing (#292)
description: A giant-style swing around the bar performed in the opposite direction to a normal giant.

name: V-Vylet
description: An entry move where the body forms a V shape, serving as a prep for the shrimp flip and bar kip to L-sit.

name: Hands-Outside Legs-Between Spin
description: A bar move with hands on the outside and legs in between, spinning around the bar.

name: Knee Dismount 180
description: A knee dismount from the bar with a 180-degree spin before landing.

name: Giant 360 Twist
description: A giant swing around the bar with a 360-degree twist performed while still holding the bar, regripping to swing straight.

name: Beginner Bar Prep Step
description: A basic first-step foundational bar move leading toward tricks #261 and #283.

name: Freestyle Bar Trick 298
description: Freestyle bar trick #298 (see video).

name: Straight-Body Hand Transfer
description: A forward transfer between bars performed with a fully straightened swing, catching the front bar solely with the hands.

name: Backflip Regrab
description: A backflip released from the bar that is caught again on the same bar mid-rotation.

name: Knee Slip to Other Side
description: A move slipping from under the knees to transfer to the other side of the bar while staying up.

name: Flip Dismount Over Head
description: A hard flipping dismount that rotates over the head to a standing landing.

name: Under-Rotated 180
description: A follow-up to #292 that is under-rotated, adding an easy 180-degree turn to face the other direction.

name: Dislocate Grip Nalet
description: A nalet (bar entry) move started from a dislocated grip, adding difficulty and discomfort to the basic version.

name: Backward Undergrip Semi-Giant
description: A backward semi-giant swing around the bar performed in an undergrip.

name: P-Bar Kip
description: A bar kip performed on parallel bars, kicking the legs to clear the front bar and rise from a hang.

name: Off Axis Flip
description: The easiest off-axis flip on the bar, throwing the body off its rotational axis.

name: Feet-Over Bar Lean Back
description: A follow-up to #292 where the feet pass over the bar and the body immediately leans back into an inverted hang position.

name: Freestyle Bar Prep Move
description: Freestyle bar trick #309 (see video).

name: P-Bar to High Bar Transfer
description: A transfer moving from parallel bars onto a regular high bar, based on trick #26 with a 90-degree turn.

name: Basic Bar Spin Entry
description: An easy foundational bar spin move that leads into harder variations such as #119.

name: Behind The Head Catch
description: A release move caught behind the head on the bar, with an option to release sooner and land on the back.

name: Straddle Bar Spin
description: A variation of trick #167 spinning around the bar with the legs spread in a straddle position.

name: Dynamic P-Bar Transition
description: A dynamic pushing transition on parallel bars, a harder version of trick #128.

name: Parallel Bar Leg-Over Swing
description: On high parallel bars, a swing that carries the legs over the farther bar.

name: One-Leg Knee Swing
description: A single-leg knee-hang swing on the bar, hanging from one leg before swinging up.

name: Jump to Handstand Fail Trick
description: A move invented from jumping toward a handstand push-up and intentionally falling over into a landing.

name: Twisted Spin Out
description: A move starting from an awkward twisted position on the bar, spinning out to a landing.

name: Airtime Swing Catch
description: A release move with brief airtime that is caught again on the bar with a bit of swing.

name: Dislocate Grip Hybrid
description: A dislocated-grip bar move combining elements of two prior tricks, requiring shoulder flexibility.

name: Off Axis Swing Gainer to Feet
description: An off-axis swinging gainer flip from the bar that rotates and lands on the feet.

name: Compression Leg Pass Through
description: A forward-falling move requiring strong compression to shove the legs through between the arms and the bar.

name: Swing 180 Release Regrab
description: A swinging release into a 180-degree turn, clapping in the air and regrabbing the bar.

name: High Bar Speedstep Release
description: From a speedstep position atop the high bar, the athlete releases and clears the bar to catch it again below.

name: Overhand Backflip Dismount
description: A dismount from the bar performed by flipping backward over the head in an overhand grip and releasing to land on the feet.
